paper.title: The Marginal Value of Adaptive Gradient Methods in Machine Learning

claims.text: Adaptive gradient methods (including Adam) generalize worse than SGD: higher test error even with better training performance.
On a constructed linearly separable problem, SGD achieves zero test error while Adam/AdaGrad/RMSProp approach 50% test error.
claims.metric: test error on synthetic separable data

claims.text: Tuned SGD matches or beats tuned adaptive methods on all tested deep learning tasks; adaptive methods do not remove learning-rate tuning.

methods.name: Linearly separable counterexample
methods.description: Construct a synthetic linearly separable binary classification problem with sparse informative features and many redundant dimensions; train linear classifiers with SGD vs Adam; compare test error to show adaptive methods generalize worse.
methods.runnable_hint: Generate the synthetic separable dataset in numpy; implement SGD and Adam for a linear classifier; train both to near-zero training error; report test error for each. Metric: test_error_sgd vs test_error_adam.
